pattern: This is a two-step mechanical transform -- filter out the zero digits to build one new number, then multiply it by the sum of its own digits -- so the whole task is just careful digit extraction done twice.
hints: First build x by walking n's digits in order and keeping only the ones that are not zero; if every digit was zero, x is 0.
Then compute the digit sum of x itself, not of the original n -- note x may have fewer digits than n did.
Multiply x by that digit sum and return the result; watch out for n = 0, whose only digit is zero, so x also ends up 0.

edge_inputs.description: n has no zero digits at all
n is entirely zero digits after a leading one
n at the maximum allowed value
zeros scattered throughout the digits, not just trailing
a single non-zero digit surrounded by zeros on both sides
the digit sum of the filtered number itself has multiple digits

stress.description: random n across the full constraint range, including many zero digits

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def sumAndMultiply(self, n):
        s = str(n)
        nz = [c for c in s if c != '0']
        x = int(''.join(nz)) if nz else 0
        digit_sum = sum(int(c) for c in str(x))
        return x * digit_sum
